The CTD pyramid · drag to orbit
The approach · Built like a pyramid

Every submission rises in five modules.

The Common Technical Document is how the world files a drug. It is also how I think about my work, from the foundation up.

Module 01

Standing

MS Regulatory Affairs at Northeastern, GPA 4.00, on a Bachelor of Pharmacy.

Module 02

Overview

Three internships across submissions, quality, safety, and regulatory strategy.

Module 03

Quality / CMC

eCTD Module 3 and DMF Type II, container-closure and stability per ICH Q-series.

Module 04

Evidence

GMP rotation, CAPA tracking, audit prep, and ALCOA+ data integrity.

Module 05

Clinical

Informed consent design, pharmacovigilance, ICH E2B(R3), and MedDRA.

Jurisdiction

Prepared across five authorities.

Submissions and query responses prepared across the agencies that decide whether a product ships. Each card links to the official agency site.

Experience · On the record

Where the work was done.

VigiSolvo (ThePharmaDaily)
Medical Writing & Regulatory Operations Intern
Remote · Jan 2024 - Mar 2025

Pharmacovigilance & Documentation

  • Helped compile pharmacovigilance and biobanking documentation across 2 CRO-affiliated projects, organizing 50+ regulatory records under GxP-aligned procedures.
  • Supported QC review of PV case documentation against ICH E2B(R3) data elements, developing a working understanding of ICSR compilation and MedDRA coding conventions.

SOP Authoring & Process Improvement

  • Co-developed a pharmacovigilance SOP for case-intake software under senior guidance, contributing to a 20% reduction in documentation errors within 3 months.
  • Drafted a regulatory intelligence newsletter summarizing monthly FDA, EMA, and ICH guideline updates for internal distribution.

Presentations & Communication

  • Presented regulatory and scientific research at 3+ national conferences, earning recognition for technical clarity across pharmacovigilance and drug regulatory topics.
  • Assisted in preparing regulatory presentation decks for internal stakeholder briefings, learning how to structure compliance findings for executive review.
  • Helped coordinate a regulatory intelligence briefing involving senior leadership, including the CEO, contributing to agenda preparation and follow-up documentation.

Training & Knowledge Transfer

  • Helped design a 7-module GxP and regulatory affairs training course covering ICH fundamentals and submission pathways, delivered to incoming intern cohorts under senior guidance.

Leadership & Outreach

  • Served as inaugural Campus Ambassador, representing the organization at 5+ academic and professional regulatory forums and supporting outreach coordination with external stakeholders.
Pharmacovigilance DocumentationICH E2B(R3)ICSR CompilationMedDRA CodingSOP DraftingRegulatory IntelligenceConference PresentationTraining DesignGxP Compliance
Symbiotec Pharma Labs & Knovea Pharmaceutical
Regulatory Affairs & Quality Assurance Intern
Indore, India · Mar 2025 - Aug 2025

Regulatory Query Responses

  • Helped draft 10+ regulatory query responses addressing FDA CMC deficiencies for API-focused ANDA filings, working alongside QA, QC, and manufacturing teams.
  • Assisted in preparing and updating query response documents for FDA, EMA, Health Canada, and PMDA requirements under RA oversight.
  • Consolidated cross-functional input from QA, QC, and Manufacturing into structured CMC deficiency responses, learning how submission-ready documentation takes shape across teams.
  • Supported documentation alignment across 2+ partnered manufacturing and API supply chain organizations, harmonizing templates and helping prepare consistent CMC document sets.

Document Review & Regulatory Intelligence

  • Reviewed 60+ regulatory and lifecycle documents, flagging labeling discrepancies and stability protocol gaps and escalating findings to senior QA staff.
  • Helped monitor FDA, EMA, and ICH regulatory intelligence across 2+ API product lines, summarizing relevant updates for RA review.

eCTD Submissions & DMF Documentation

  • Contributed to CTD/eCTD-ready documentation for 2 API-focused hormone and corticosteroid projects, organizing 30+ CMC documents across Modules 2.3 and 3.2.
  • Worked alongside the regulatory team on eCTD Module 3 compilation for 2+ ANDA submissions, verifying PDF formatting, bookmarks, and pagination, with hands-on exposure to Veeva Vault RIM.
  • Supported DMF (Type II) documentation under senior guidance, assembling stability summaries and analytical method validation reports into CTD Module 3.2.S format.

Medical Device & Combination Products

  • Supported review of dual-chamber vial system CMC considerations, including stability, container-closure integrity, and labeling, gaining early exposure to how combination product regulatory logic differs from standard drug filings.

Technical Regulatory Knowledge

  • Reviewed nitrosamine impurity risk assessment frameworks against FDA and EMA guidance, summarizing control strategy expectations for API and finished dosage form filings.
  • Completed a structured self-review of ICH Q1-Q12 and CTD Module 3 expectations, building a regulatory foundation applied across later project work.

Quality & Compliance

  • Completed an 8-week GMP training rotation across QC, QA, Production, Warehouse, and Regulatory, assisting with change control and CAPA tracking under 21 CFR 210/211.
  • Examined batch manufacturing and packaging records against master batch records and SOPs under QA supervision, applying ALCOA+ data integrity principles to flag transcription errors.
  • Helped develop a replica SOP applying document control standards including version numbering, effective dating, and approval routing, reviewed by the QA manager.
  • Assisted in organizing controlled documents and maintaining traceability matrices for internal and external quality audits.

Team Lead, Strategic Planning & AI Automation

Strategic Planning & Leadership

  • Explored AI and automation tools to streamline documentation workflows, delivering a final stakeholder presentation summarizing project outcomes and implementation recommendations.
